info.title: syncdesk_api
info.description: Backend e API GATEWAY para o projeto SyncDesk

post.description: Validates credentials and returns a pair of access / refresh tokens. Returns 401 for wrong email or password.

200.description: Login successful. Returns access and refresh tokens.

post.description: Creates a new user account with the default 'user' role and returns the created user along with access / refresh tokens. Returns 400 if the email is already taken.
Rotates the refresh token and issues a new access token. The previous refresh token is invalidated.

200.description: Session refreshed. Returns new access and refresh tokens.

401.description: Invalid or expired session. A new login is required.

post.description: Revokes the current session so that the associated tokens can no longer be used.

get.description: Returns the profile of the currently authenticated user, including assigned roles.

post.summary: Register a new user as admin
post.description: Creates a new user account via admin permissions. Returns 400 when registration data is invalid (for example, duplicate email).
Changes the authenticated user's password after validating the current password.
Triggers the password reset flow for a given email. For security, the endpoint returns success even when the email is not registered.

200.description: Password reset flow triggered (always returns success).

post.description: Resets the user's password using a valid reset token. Returns 400 when the token is invalid, expired, or cannot be used.

400.description: Invalid token or password reset could not be completed.

get.description: Returns every role registered in the system.

200.description: List of all roles retrieved successfully.

post.description: Creates a new role. Returns 409 if the name is already taken.

409.description: A role with this name already exists.

get.description: Returns a single role by its ID. Returns 404 if not found.

put.description: Fully replaces the role identified by ID. Returns 404 if not found.

patch.description: Applies a partial update to the role identified by ID. Returns 404 if not found.

delete.description: Deletes the role identified by ID. Returns 404 if not found.

get.description: Returns the role and its associated permissions. Returns 404 if not found.

200.description: Role with associated permissions retrieved successfully.

post.description: Associates the given permission IDs with the role.

200.description: Permissions added to the role successfully.

get.description: Returns every permission registered in the system.

200.description: List of all permissions retrieved successfully.

post.description: Creates a new permission. Returns 409 if the name is already taken.

409.description: A permission with this name already exists.

get.description: Returns a single permission by its ID. Returns 404 if not found.

put.description: Fully replaces the permission identified by ID. Returns 404 if not found.

patch.description: Applies a partial update to the permission identified by ID. Returns 404 if not found.

delete.description: Deletes the permission identified by ID. Returns 404 if not found.

get.description: Returns the permission and its associated roles. Returns 404 if not found.

200.description: Permission with associated roles retrieved successfully.

post.description: Associates the permission with the given role IDs.

200.description: Permission added to the specified roles successfully.

get.description: Returns every user registered in the system.

200.description: List of all users retrieved successfully.

post.description: Creates a new user (admin-only). Returns 409 if the email is already taken.

409.description: A user with this email already exists.

get.description: Returns a single user by their UUID. Returns 404 if not found.

put.description: Fully replaces the user identified by UUID. Returns 404 if not found.

patch.description: Applies a partial update to the user identified by UUID. Returns 404 if not found.

post.description: Associates the given role IDs with the user. Returns 400 if no role IDs are provided, 404 if user or roles not found.

404.description: User or one of the referenced roles not found.

get.description: Returns all conversations associated with the given client id. Requires the 'chat:read' permission.

200.description: List of conversations for the client retrieved successfully.

403.description: The authenticated user does not have permission to read chats.

get.description: Returns all conversations associated with the given ticket. Non-admin users must be a current participant in the ticket's latest conversation.

403.description: The authenticated user is not a participant in this ticket.

get.summary: Get paginated messages for a ticket
get.description: Retrieves paginated messages across all conversations of the given ticket. Messages are ordered chronologically in descending order. Non-admin users must be a current participant in the ticket.

403.description: The authenticated user is not a participant in this ticket.

post.description: Creates a new live-chat conversation linked to a ticket. Non-agent / non-admin users can only create conversations for themselves. Returns 409 if a conversation with the same unique constraints already exists.

403.description: User attempted to create a conversation on behalf of another user.

409.description: A conversation with the same constraints already exists.

patch.summary: Assign an agent to a conversation
patch.description: Sets or reassigns the agent for an existing conversation. Only admins or the currently assigned agent can reassign. The target agent_id must correspond to a user with the 'agent' role.

200.description: Agent assigned to the conversation successfully.

403.description: The user is not allowed to reassign, or the provided agent_id does not belong to a valid agent.

404.description: Conversation or referenced resource not found.

get.description: root endpoint to verify the API's heakth status.

client_id.title: Client Id
client_id.description: Identifier of the client user in the auth domain.

type.title: Type
type.description: A URI reference that identifies the problem type.

title.title: Title
title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type.

status.title: Status
status.description: The HTTP status code generated by the origin server for this occurrence of the problem.

detail.title: Detail
detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.

instance.title: Instance
instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.

errors.title: Errors
errors.description: Additional error details, such as validation issues.

meta.description: Metadata about the error response (timestamp, status, etc.).

ErrorContent.title: ErrorContent
ErrorContent.description: Standardized error response format, based on RFC 7807 (Problem Details for HTTP APIs).

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: Timestamp when the response was generated, in ISO 8601 format.

success.title: Success
success.description: Indicates the overall status of the response (e.g., 'success' or 'error').

request_id.title: Request Id
request_id.description: Optional unique identifier for tracking the request.

Meta.title: Meta
Meta.description: Metadata about the response, such as timestamp, status, and optional request identifiers.
